calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Accounting Of Reserve By Kelly Dixon
Off Calendar Nov-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Vann for and with Jessie Jones and Jessie Turner. Jason Louie for Franchesta Grove.

The Court stated that it was not inclined to charge Mr. Vann's attorneys fees as a surcharge against Ms. Grove but that it would charge them as a surcharge against Kelly Dixon (Ms. Grove's former Attorney) and that Mr. Vann would have to file an amended petition with personal notice to Ms. Dixon if he wanted to pursue it. After conferring with his Clients, Mr. Vann stated that his Clients did not want to pursue the surcharge issue. Mr. Louie stated that the Texas property matter is proceeding. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: J. Chien.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Accounting Of Reserve By Kelly Dixon
Off Calendar Oct-25-2010 Continued To Nov-22-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Vann for and with Jessie F. Jones and Jessie N. Turner. Jason Louie for Franchesta Grove.

The matter was continued to November 22, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Louie to file a response to Mr. Vann's objections.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Accounting Of Reserve By Kelly Dixon
Off Calendar Oct-04-2010 Continued To Oct-25-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar Oct-04-2010 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Accounting Of Reserve By Kelly Dixon
Off Calendar Aug-30-2010 Continued To Oct-04-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Vann for and with Jessie Jones. Jason Louie for Franchesta Grove.

Mr. Louie stated that he made significant efforts by facsimile, telephone and mail to get Ms. Dixon to sign the Substitution of Attorney and that he received no response what so ever. The Court will accept the Substitution of Attorney without Kelly Dixon's signature. Mr. Louie also stated that they identified three or four properties in Texas, that the process for the ancillary proceeding in Texas was as simple as providing certified copies of the documents here by an ex parte basis but that an inventory and appraisal was required and that they were having trouble defining the properties. Furthermore, Mr. Louie stated that they were working cooperativley on the ancillary proceeding. Mr. Vann stated that he did not have the opportunity to review the accounting, requested a continuance and stated that one of the pieces of property may be an heirship property. The matter was continued to October 4, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to address the issues of the accounting, the ancillary proceeding in Texas, Mr. Vann's attorney's fees and sanctions. Sanctions against Ms. Grove stopped running as of August 26, 2010.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-30-2010 Continued To Oct-04-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Accounting Of Reserve By Kelly Dixon
Off Calendar Aug-09-2010 Continued To Aug-30-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jason Louie for Franchesta Grove. Dennis Vann for Jessie Jones and Jesie Nell Turner.

Mr. Louie stated that he had just been retained and that he would file the Substitution of Attorney by the end of this week. Mr. Vann stated that he had no response to either requests and that he was not able to speak to Ms. Grove at the last hearing. Mr. Louie requested a four week continuance stating that Ms. Grove was not able to communicate with her prior Counsel, asked that sanctions be stopped on the day of filing of the accounting petition, stated that there were two small pieces of property in Texas and that the costs outweigh the value. In addition, Mr. Louie stated that his Client was willing to give her rights to the properties over to the other Beneficiaries. The matter was continued to August 30, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Various Bailiffs.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-09-2010 Continued To Aug-30-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Accounting Of Reserve By Kelly Dixon
Off Calendar Jul-12-2010 Continued To Aug-09-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Vann for and with Jessie Jones. Franchesta Grove in Propria Persona.

The Court informed Ms. Grove that an account on the cash reserve needed to be filed. Ms. Grove stated that she was trying to meet with Kelly Dixon, that she was unable to reach Ms. Dixon and that she was going over to Ms. Dixon's office after Court. Once again the Court reminded Ms. Grove that the accounting was needed and that status on the ancillary proceeding in Texas was also needed. The Court also informed Ms. Grove that sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day continue to run and suggested substituting in other Counsel. The matter was continued to August 9, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Y. Watts.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-12-2010 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-12-2010 Continued To Aug-09-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Accounting Of Reserve By Kelly Dixon
Off Calendar Jun-07-2010 Continued To Jul-12-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Vann for and with Jessie Faye Jones.

Mr. Vann stated that he had not heard from Kelly Dixon and that he did not get an accounting on the reserve. An Order to Show Cause will be issued to the Executor Franchesta Grove for failure to file the account on the reserve and to show cause why she should not be surcharged for Attorney Dennis Vann's fees and costs. Sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day will be imposed against Franchesta Grove if the account on the reserve is not filed by close of business July 7, 2010. The return date on the Order to Show Cause is July 12, 2010 at 9:00 a.m, in Dept. 204. Mr. Vann is to prepare the Order to Show Cause.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-07-2010 Continued To Jul-12-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Accounting Of Reserve By Kelly Dixon
Off Calendar Apr-26-2010 Continued To Jun-07-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Vann for and Jessie Jones. Kelly Dixon for Franchesta Grove.

Ms. Dixon stated that she has attempted to contact her Client since November, that she got no response, that she informed her Client that she may have to file a Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel and therefore have to appear before the Court on her own, and suggested a short continuance or issuing an Order to Appear. In addition, Ms. Dixon stated that she believed that there was no ancillary proceeding in Texas. The matter was continued to June 7, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Vann will prepare an Order to Show Cause to Franchesta Grove to appear on June 7, 2010 in the event that the accounting on the $25,000.00 reserve and a status report of the ancillary proceeding in Texas if any is not filed by June 2, 2010.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition For Request For Accounting On Reserve And Request For Report On Ancillary Proceedings And For Award Of Cosgts And Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Mar-26-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Vann for and with Jessie Jones.

Mr. Vann stated that he had written letters and made numerous phone calls to Ms. Dixon but that he got no response. In addition, he stated that Ms. Dixon represented that there was an ancillary proceeding in Texas, that he did a search in Henderson County and that he found nothing, and that there was no account on the reserve. The Court directed Mr. Vann to carve out the aspect of the petition with regard to the request for Attorney's fees and costs stating that it is to be addressed at a later hearing. The Court set a date for Ms. Dixon Attorney for Franchesta Grove to file an accounting of the reserve and a report by April 9, 2010 with a hearing date of April 26, 2010. The Court granted the petition (subject to a written order). Mr. Vann is to prepare the order.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Clauzel.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-28-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Vann for and with Jessie Jones. Kelly Dixon for Franchesta Grove (Executor).

Ms. Dixon addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes, stated that the receipts for the 5th Preliminary distribution had been filed last week and that she had just received the receipts from Mr. Vann and Ms. Jones which were going to be filed. The parties presented argument regarding Ms. Jones having to pay a penalty in the amount of $1,286.00 for late closing on the property she purchased, Ms. Jones enetering into the purchase agreement without having funds, attorney's fees, fees for the Texas proceeding, extra ordinary fees and as to the estate being ready to be closed. After hearing oral argument, the Court took the matter under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Payments Of Preliminary Distribution Ordered On 5/30/08
Off Calendar Jun-24-2008 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-17-2008 Continued To Jul-16-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Vann for and with Jessie Nell Turner and Jessie Jones. Kelly Dixon for Franchesta Grove (Executor).

Mr. Vann stated that the order for preliminary distribution had been signed, but that his client had not received the funds. In addition, he stated that his client needed the funds as escrow was closing. Ms. Dixon stated that her client had not sent the checks, that it was difficult to reach her due to her job and that she was going to try to get the money wire transferred. The issue regarding the preliminary distribution was set for June 24, 2008. Sanctions may be imposed if losses are suffered. The Court continued the accounting petition to July 16, 2008. Ms. Dixon is to file the amendment by June 27, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Anderson.

Petition For Fifth Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar May-30-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Vann for Jessie Jones and Jessie Turner. Kelly Dixon for Franchesta Grove.

The Court stated that there were no reciepts for the prior preliminary distribution and no responses to the May 7th Examiner's notes. Ms. Dixon stated that she had filed the receipts on Friday, that she had prepared notes and that she had spoken to the Examiner. Mr. Vann objected to the $100,000.00 reserve, stated that his client needed her distribution and stated that he had not reviewed Ms. Dixon's response. Ms. Dixon is to file a declaration in regard to the outstanding issues, (receipts, estate taxes, extraordinary fees). The parties stipulated to a preliminary distribution being made to all the heirs. Mr. Vann will prepare an order. The Petition For Preliminary Distribution was taken under submission. The Accounting Petition was continued to June 17, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Giselle Casey CSR#8098. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Neu.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Vann for Jessie Jones and Jessie Niel Turner. Kelly Dixon for Franchesta Grove (Executor).

Ms. Dixon stated that she was ready to file the First and Final account, and that the conveyance of the property in Texas was still in process. Mr. Vann expressed that his client was tired of waiting, and that Ms. Dixon's client was trying to delay the final distribution. The matter was continued to March 24, 2008 for review of the supplemantal account and amendment, and the petition for final distribution.

Off Calendar Dec-17-2007 Continued To Feb-25-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES- ATTY. KELLY DIXON FOR THE EXECUTOR; ATTY. DENNIS VANN APPEARING FOR JESSIE JONES AND JESSIE TURNER (BENEFICIARIES);

ATTY. DIXON REPORTED TO THE COURT THE REASON WHY AN AMENDMENT TO A FINAL ACCOUNT AND REPORT HAD NOT BEEN FILED. THE ACCOUNTANT IS IN LONG BEACH AND IS NOT IN CLOSE PROXIMITY. THE ACCOUNTANT IS NOT DOING THE ACCOUNTING IN THE EXACT FORMAT FOR HE IS NOT A PROBATE ACCOUNTANT. THIS ALSO INVOLVE A TEXAS PROPERTY WHICH REQUIRES AN APOSTILE PROCEEDING. ATTY. VANN REPORTED THAT HE HAS NOT FILED ANY OBJECTION YET SINCE HE DID NOT GET ANY RESPONSE FROM HIS LETTER TO MS. DIXON. THE COURT CONTINUED THE MATTER TO FEBRUARY 25, 2007 FOR THE AMENDED PETITION.
